story.iWant: domain entities for tickets (base, incident, service request) with proper JPA mappings, UUIDv7 primary keys, and optimistic locking
story.soThat: we can persist ticket data with time-ordered IDs, version control, and inheritance support for Epic 2 ITSM features

tasks.task: Create Ticket base entity and enums
Integration tests - Optimistic locking and queries

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Create Ticket base entity with UUIDv7 primary key using @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7), fields: id, ticketType, title, description, status, priority, category, requesterId, assigneeId, version (@Version), createdAt, updatedAt with @PrePersist/@PreUpdate lifecycle callbacks
Create Incident entity extending Ticket with additional fields: severity, resolutionNotes, resolvedAt; constructor sets ticketType=INCIDENT
Create ServiceRequest entity extending Ticket with additional fields: requestType, approvalStatus, fulfillerId, fulfilledAt; constructor sets ticketType=SERVICE_REQUEST
Create TicketComment entity with UUIDv7 ID, ticketId FK, author, commentText, isInternal, createdAt with @PrePersist lifecycle callback
Create RoutingRule entity with UUIDv7 ID, ruleName, conditionType, conditionValue, targetTeamId, targetAgentId, priority, enabled
Create Spring Data JPA repositories: TicketRepository, IncidentRepository, ServiceRequestRepository, TicketCommentRepository, RoutingRuleRepository with custom query methods

Create enum types: TicketType (INCIDENT, SERVICE_REQUEST), TicketStatus (NEW, ASSIGNED, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, CLOSED), Priority (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW), ApprovalStatus (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED), ConditionType (CATEGORY, PRIORITY, SUBCATEGORY, ROUND_ROBIN)
All entities follow Spring Modulith conventions: internal/domain package, Lombok annotations, Javadoc with @since 2.1 tag
Integration test: Ticket persistence, retrieval, UUIDv7 time-ordering verification
Integration test: Incident subclass persistence with severity and resolutionNotes
Integration test: ServiceRequest subclass persistence with approvalStatus
Integration test: Optimistic locking with concurrent updates (verify OptimisticLockException)
Integration test: TicketComment persistence and chronological query
Integration test: RoutingRule query by enabled and priority order
Load test: Bulk insert 1000 tickets in <5s, verify UUIDv7 time-ordering, p99 latency <50ms
Load test: 500 concurrent reads + 50 writes/sec for 5 minutes, p95 read <10ms, write <50ms, zero OptimisticLockExceptions

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Technical Specification
doc.section: Technical Details - Ticket Domain Entity (UUIDv7 Primary Keys)
doc.snippet: Lines 139-263: Complete Ticket.java entity definition with UUIDv7, @Version, lifecycle callbacks, inheritance strategy discussion
doc.relevance: Authoritative source for all entity requirements, field definitions, JPA annotations, and implementation patterns

doc.title: UUIDv7 Implementation Guide
doc.snippet: Complete guide on @UuidGenerator annotation, performance characteristics (6.6x faster inserts vs UUIDv4), time-ordering properties
doc.relevance: Required for AC1 UUIDv7 implementation; explains annotation usage and benefits

doc.title: Module Boundaries
doc.snippet: ITSM module location: io.monosense.synergyflow.itsm; internal/ for domain entities (package-private); spi/ for cross-module interfaces
doc.relevance: Defines package structure for AC8; enforces Spring Modulith conventions

doc.title: Data Model v1
doc.snippet: TICKETS table structure with UUID primary keys, INCIDENTS and SERVICE_REQUESTS inheritance, read models (ticket_card)
doc.relevance: Shows expected database schema, confirms UUIDv7 policy for all primary keys

doc.title: Architecture Decision Records
doc.snippet: Rationale for UUIDv7 choice, Testcontainers integration test requirements
doc.relevance: Context for UUIDv7 decision, testing strategy guidance for IT1-IT7

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Tech Spec - Implementation Guide
doc.section: Week 3-4: Ticket CRUD + Routing Engine (Sprint 3) - Story 2.1
doc.snippet: Lines 1388-1398: Story 2.1 breakdown with 8 story points, tasks list, acceptance criteria summary
doc.relevance: Sprint context, story points estimate, original task breakdown

artifact.reason: Existing Ticket entity from Epic 1 uses manual UUID generation (UUID.randomUUID()) and String fields for status/priority. Story 2.1 requires: (1) Replace with @UuidGenerator for UUIDv7, (2) Convert status/priority to enums, (3) Add ticketType enum field, (4) Add @PrePersist/@PreUpdate callbacks, (5) Prepare for inheritance (Incident, ServiceRequest subclasses)

changes.change: Replace UUID id manual generation with @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7)
Add ticketType field with TicketType enum (INCIDENT, SERVICE_REQUEST)
Convert status String to TicketStatus enum (NEW, ASSIGNED, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, CLOSED)
Convert priority String to Priority enum (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
Add @PrePersist/@PreUpdate lifecycle callbacks for timestamp management
Add incrementVersion() public method for explicit version bumps
Add @Inheritance annotation to support Incident/ServiceRequest subclasses
Remove ticketNumber field (not in Story 2.1 requirements)

artifact.reason: Repository exists from Epic 1; needs custom query methods per AC6: findByStatus, findByRequesterId, findByAssigneeId
Outbox pattern interface from Epic 1 Story 1.5; used by TicketService (Story 2.2) to publish events, no changes needed for Story 2.1
Outbox table entity from Story 1.5; TicketService will write domain events here (Story 2.2), no changes needed for Story 2.1
Domain event from Epic 1; may need fields updated to match new Ticket entity structure (ticketType, status/priority enums)
Domain event for ticket assignment, used in Story 2.2 (TicketService), no changes needed for Story 2.1
Read model projection for ticket queue view (Epic 1 Story 1.6); will be updated by Event Worker when ticket domain events published (Story 2.2+), no direct changes in Story 2.1

dependency.name: hibernate-core
dependency.note: Required for @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7) annotation - Hibernate 7.0+ native UUIDv7 support

dependency.name: testcontainers-postgresql
dependency.note: Required for integration tests IT1-IT7 with PostgreSQL container

dependency.name: spring-boot-testcontainers
dependency.note: Spring Boot Testcontainers support for @ServiceConnection annotation

dependency.name: archunit-junit5
dependency.note: For Spring Modulith modularity tests (enforce internal package access rules)

constraint.rule: All domain entities must be placed in internal/domain/ package with package-private visibility (no public modifier on class)
constraint.impact: Enforces encapsulation; prevents direct access from other modules

constraint.rule: All repositories must be placed in internal/repository/ package with package-private visibility
constraint.impact: Service layer (internal/service/) accesses repositories, but external modules cannot

constraint.rule: All primary keys must use UUIDv7 with @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7) annotation
constraint.impact: Ensures time-ordered IDs, better index locality, 6.6x faster inserts vs UUIDv4

constraint.rule: All write entities must include @Version field for optimistic locking
constraint.impact: Prevents lost updates from concurrent modifications; JPA auto-increments version on save

constraint.rule: Timestamp fields must use Instant type (not LocalDateTime or Date)
constraint.impact: Timezone-agnostic UTC timestamps; maps to PostgreSQL timestamptz

constraint.rule: Entity timestamps must be managed via @PrePersist and @PreUpdate lifecycle callbacks (not @CreationTimestamp/@UpdateTimestamp)
constraint.impact: Explicit control over timestamp logic; testable without database

constraint.rule: Use Lombok @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor for boilerplate reduction, but provide explicit constructors for aggregate creation
constraint.impact: Reduces code duplication while maintaining domain logic clarity

constraint.rule: All public methods must have Javadoc with @since 2.1 tag
constraint.impact: Documents API evolution for future stories

constraint.rule: Integration tests must use Testcontainers PostgreSQL, not H2 or in-memory databases
constraint.impact: Tests run against real PostgreSQL; catches database-specific issues

constraint.rule: Integration tests must be tagged with @Tag("integration") and excluded from default test runs (only run explicitly)
constraint.impact: Avoids slow container startup during rapid development; run via ./gradlew test -DincludeTags=integration

constraint.rule: Load tests must be tagged with @Tag("load") and excluded from CI pipeline (run manually or nightly)
constraint.impact: Prevents long-running load tests from blocking CI; run via ./gradlew test -DincludeTags=load

constraint.rule: JPA inheritance strategy decision deferred to implementation: choose JOINED (normalized) or SINGLE_TABLE (faster reads); document choice in code comments with rationale
constraint.impact: Developer discretion; must document trade-offs (JOINED: cleaner schema, SINGLE_TABLE: faster polymorphic queries)

interface.name: EventPublisher
interface.usage: Used by TicketService (Story 2.2) to publish domain events (TicketCreated, TicketAssigned, etc.) to outbox table; not directly used in Story 2.1 entity layer

interface.name: JpaRepository
interface.usage: All repositories extend JpaRepository for CRUD operations + custom query methods; provides save(), findById(), findAll(), etc.

standards.paragraph: Testing follows Spring Boot Test framework with Testcontainers for integration tests. Unit tests use JUnit 5 with no mocking (pure POJO tests for lifecycle callbacks and helper methods). Integration tests use @DataJpaTest for repository layer tests or @SpringBootTest for full context tests. All integration tests must use Testcontainers PostgreSQL (not H2) per ADR-009. Tests are tagged with @Tag("integration") or @Tag("load") and excluded from default runs. Coverage target: >80% for domain logic. Load tests measure performance targets: LT1 requires >200 inserts/sec with p99 <50ms, LT2 requires sustained 500 reads/sec + 50 writes/sec with p95 read <10ms and write <50ms.

locations.note: Unit tests mirror source structure in test/java; integration tests in separate integration/ package

idea.test: TicketTest.testPrePersistSetsTimestamps() - Create Ticket, manually invoke onCreate(), verify createdAt and updatedAt both set to non-null Instant
TicketTest.testPreUpdateUpdatesTimestamp() - Create Ticket, invoke onCreate(), wait 10ms, invoke onUpdate(), verify updatedAt > createdAt
TicketTest.testIncrementVersion() - Create Ticket with version=1L, call incrementVersion(), verify version==2L
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testTicketPersistenceAndRetrieval() - Save Ticket to DB, retrieve by ID, verify all fields match
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testUuidV7TimeOrdering() - Create two Tickets sequentially, verify ID1 < ID2 lexicographically (UUIDv7 time-ordering property)
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testIncidentSubclassPersistence() - Save Incident with severity=CRITICAL, resolutionNotes="Rebooted server", retrieve, verify subclass fields and ticketType
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testServiceRequestSubclassPersistence() - Save ServiceRequest with approvalStatus=PENDING, retrieve, verify subclass fields and ticketType
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testOptimisticLocking() - Save Ticket (version=0), load in two transactions, update+save in tx1 (version→1), update+save in tx2 → expect OptimisticLockException
TicketCommentIntegrationTest.testCommentPersistenceAndChronologicalQuery() - Create Ticket, add 3 comments with different timestamps, query by ticketId, verify chronological order (createdAt ASC)

RoutingRuleIntegrationTest.testQueryEnabledRulesByPriority() - Create 3 rules with priority=1,2,3 enabled=true, 1 rule enabled=false, query findByEnabledTrueOrderByPriorityAsc(), verify 3 rules returned in correct order
TicketEntityIntegrationTest.testLifecycleCallbacks() - Create Ticket, save, verify createdAt populated; wait 100ms, update title, save, verify updatedAt > createdAt

TicketEntityLoadTest.testBulkTicketCreation() - Insert 1000 tickets in loop, measure total time (<5s), calculate throughput (>200/sec), measure p99 latency (<50ms per insert), verify all UUIDs time-ordered with ≤1% inversions

TicketEntityLoadTest.testConcurrentReadWrite() - Spawn 10 read threads (50 reads/sec each = 500 total) + 5 write threads (10 writes/sec each = 50 total), run for 5 minutes, measure p95 latency (read <10ms, write <50ms), verify zero OptimisticLockExceptions, verify connection pool stable
